name: universal-design-principles
description: A working library of 42 framework-agnostic design principles drawn from Universal Principles of Design (Lidwell, Holden, Butler, 2003). Five composable plugins covering perception, cognition, interaction, aesthetics, and process — each principle ships as a router skill plus context-specific sub-skills with deep-dive references.

plugins.name: perception-and-hierarchy-principles
plugins.description: Visual perception, hierarchy, layout, and the structuring of attention. Covers Hierarchy, Proximity, Signal-to-Noise Ratio, Color, Alignment, Legibility, Readability, Gestalt Similarity, Closure, and Figure-Ground Relationship.

plugins.name: cognition-and-learnability-principles
plugins.description: Mental models, complexity reduction, memory, and learnability. Covers Hick's Law, Wayfinding, Progressive Disclosure, 80/20 Rule, Chunking, Mental Model, Recognition Over Recall, Consistency, Mimicry, and Iconic Representation.

plugins.name: interaction-and-control-principles
plugins.description: Affordance, feedback, errors, and user control. Covers Fitts's Law, Affordance, Forgiveness, Feedback Loop, Errors, Constraint, Mapping, Expectation Effect, and Control.

plugins.name: aesthetics-and-emotion-principles
plugins.description: Beauty, perceived quality, brand personality, and emotional response. Covers Aesthetic-Usability Effect, Archetypes, Form Follows Function, Wabi-Sabi, Exposure Effect, and Immersion.

plugins.name: process-and-robustness-principles
plugins.description: Iteration, accessibility, reliability, and design process. Covers Accessibility, Iteration, Factor of Safety, Prototyping, Weakest Link, Scaling Fallacy, and Ockham's Razor.
